summary: 2026-06-07 07:10:32 PDT — The branch implements the Zoom sample pattern: `connect`/`onConnect` are removed from capabilities and `RealZoom.init()`, `_connected`/`_pendingMsg` are gone, direct sends are in place, and docs mostly match. I could not run `npm test` because this read-only sandbox blocks Vitest/Vite writing `client/node_modules/.vite-temp`.

findings.title: Synchronous postMessage throws still escape
findings.claim: AC4 says `postMessage` must never throw synchronously, but `Promise.resolve(this._sdk.postMessage(payload))` invokes the SDK before the promise chain exists. If `sdk.postMessage` throws synchronously, the error escapes, no `ok:false` log is emitted, and the caller can still crash despite the direct-send error-swallowing requirement.
findings.suggestion: Wrap the SDK call in `try/catch`, emit the `postMessage ok:false` log on caught errors, then `Promise.resolve(result).then(...).catch(...)`. Add a fake SDK case where `postMessage` throws synchronously.

findings.title: RealZoom receive path is untested
findings.claim: AC3 depends on `RealZoom.init()` registering `sdk.onMessage` and dispatching to `RealZoom.onMessage()` subscribers, but `makeFakeSdk` has no `onMessage` hook and the RealZoom bridge tests only assert direct sends. A regression deleting or breaking the inCamera receive path would still pass these tests, even though the official-sample approach relies on that receiver.
findings.suggestion: Add `onMessage(cb)` plus a `fireMessage(payload)` helper to the fake SDK, then test that after `init()`, `RealZoom.onMessage()` subscribers receive both `{ payload }` events and raw event payloads as intended.

findings.title: Stale connect bridge comment remains
findings.claim: The comment still says RealZoom is exported so the `connect/postMessage bridge` can be unit-tested. The connect bridge has been intentionally removed, so this is stale against AC5's docs/comment cleanup.
findings.suggestion: Reword it to describe the direct `postMessage`/`onMessage` bridge or the Zoom adapter integration tests without mentioning `connect`.
